methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $58M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $11.0B.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $5B

private.name: Positron AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Sagence is a Series B-stage semiconductor company founded by Vishal Sarin that has raised $58M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 11 peers spanning the Chips & Semiconductors sector: 3 public companies (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private firms including stage-adjacent players such as Etched (Series B, $5.0B), Positron AI (Series B, $1.0B), d-Matrix (Series C, $2.0B), SambaNova Systems (Series A, $11.0B), and DEEPX (Series C, $529M).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression methodology applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $58M total raised proxy, yielding $110M. Peer-median analysis across the 5 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables produces a median valuation of $2.0B, with a range spanning $529M to $11.0B. The substantial dispersion reflects heterogeneity in market positioning and capital intensity within the sector cohort.

These methods support a triangulated valuation range of $94M (low) to $2.3B (high), with a median of $1.1B. The wide range reflects tension between the conservative stage-progression approach and peer multiples that span nearly two orders of magnitude. Key uncertainties include differentiation within the semiconductor frontier-tech landscape, funding environment trajectory, and whether Sagence's competitive position aligns with upper- or lower-quartile peer valuations.
